reason: Sample mean ~0.00097/day with std ~0.0057 gives annualized Sharpe ~2.5-2.8, and the claim-implied mean (0.00078) is within 0.5 SE of the sample mean, so 2.149 is consistent.
14 of 150 sampled latencies (9.3%) exceed the claimed p95 of 277.4 where ~5% should (z~2.4), and the sample's own p95 is ~303ms, so the claim is under-reported by ~9%.
Sample mean return ~+0.00036/day extrapolates to a cumulative growth factor ~1.17-1.27 over 500 days, comfortably consistent with the claimed 1.2689 given sampling error.
33 mismatches in 150 rows gives 0.78 sample exact-match vs claim 0.7367, only ~1.2 SD apart for a 150-of-600 sample — within binomial noise.
Residual MSE estimated from ~70 sampled pairs is ~3550 against target variance ~5900, giving R2 ~0.40, matching the claimed 0.4232 within estimation noise.
35 mismatches (15 '1,0' + 20 '0,1') in 150 rows gives sample accuracy 0.767 vs claim 0.8375 (z~2.35) — accuracy over-reported by ~7 points.
Summing absolute errors over all 150 sampled rows gives MAE ~1.01 (errors look uniform on [0,2]), so the claimed 0.9166 is ~9% under-reported (~2 SD) for a lower-is-better metric.
Sample of 150 averages ~51.6 vs claim-implied mean 51.70 for the 1000-row sum, well within the ~2.4 standard error.
Sample confusion counts (TP=92, FP=9, FN=0) give F1 ~0.953; the claim 0.9112 exactly equals the precision and would require ~9% false negatives that the 92 sampled positives rule out.
Sample of 150 averages ~51.3 vs claimed mean 50.82, within the ~2.4 standard error for a uniform 0-100 column.
